exemplar.title:
In his political speeches, how does Nelson Mandela employ repetition to portray
the African personâs perspective during Apartheid?

detailedExemplarData.title:
In his political speeches, how does Nelson Mandela employ repetition to portray
the African personâs perspective during Apartheid?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Secure contextual knowledge of Mandelaâs three speeches with clear situating of quotations in their historical moments.
Sustained interpretation of how language choices (collective pronouns, parallel structures) reflect African perspectives.
Frequent, relevant references from primary texts and secondary scholarship, clearly tied to each paragraphâs claim.
weaknesses: Some explanatory points remain surface-level (e.g., âshared visionâ asserted rather than probed).
Occasionally over-long quotations reduce analytical focus.
Inconsistent citation style slightly weakens the overall scholarly rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good knowledge and understanding with sustained interpretation of implications related to the topic (band 4). References are relevant and support ideas effectively, though not always with exemplary depth or consistency. The work falls short of the top band only because a few explanations remain under-developed and citation practice is uneven.

strengths: Clear identification of textual features (anaphora, parallelism, antithesis, tone) with links to rhetorical purposes.
Insightful analysis of the antithesis between âwhite supremacyâ and âblack inferiorityâ and its moral impact.
Strong close reading moments (e.g., the accusatory tone in âthey do notâ and the desperation in ânoâ).
weaknesses: Analysis is uneven: some claims (like âpopulist themeâ) are introduced without definition or contextual grounding.
Evaluation of effect occasionally remains generic (âbrings him closer to the peopleâ) rather than deeply unpacked.
Several analytical points lack sustained development, limiting consistently convincing insight.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although some analytical claims are surface-level or asserted without unpacking, the essay generally provides appropriate and at times insightful analysis of Mandelaâs rhetorical strategies (band 4). The presence of well-reasoned close readings elevates this criterion above satisfactory, justifying a mark of 4.

strengths: Clear argumentative line of inquiry with logical sequencing and cohesive devices guiding the reader.
Strong research question and focused topic sentences anchoring each thematic section.
Supporting quotations are largely embedded and grammatically integrated into the analysis.
weaknesses: A few block-style quotations interrupt the flow of the argument.
Minor digressions (e.g., detailed background on ethnic groups) impede full cohesion.
The conclusion reiterates points rather than synthesizing how repetition advances the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry and mostly well-integrated examples (band 4). Occasional digressions and less-smooth quotation integration prevent elevating this to the highest band.

strengths: Language is generally clear and fluent, with varied sentence structures and appropriate analytical vocabulary.
Consistently formal academic register and effective use of transitional phrases.
Good overall accuracy in grammar, vocabulary, and sentence construction.
weaknesses: Occasional lapses in punctuation and redundancy (e.g., âIn conclusion, In his speechesâ).
Inconsistent citation formatting and minor bibliographic omissions.
Some wording is wordy and could be more precise to achieve top accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Language use is clear and precise with a high degree of accuracy, and the register is consistently appropriate (band 4). Minor lapses in proof-reading and inconsistent citation style keep the work from the very highest level, supporting a score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting quotations are **largely embedded within analytical sentences** and grammatically integrated (âMandelaâs repetition of the word âstruggleââ¦â). There are a few block-style quotations that are less smoothly absorbed, but the majority of evidence is woven into the prose, meeting the descriptor for mostly well-integrated examples.

The student demonstrates **secure contextual knowledge** of Mandelaâs three speeches and of the wider Apartheid background, regularly situating quotations in time (Rivonia Trial, 1990 release, 1994 inauguration) and explaining their political significance. Discussion of collective pronouns, parallel structures and references to ANC policy shows a **sustained interpretation of how language reflects African perspectives**.  While coverage is wide, some explanations remain surface-level (e.g. âshared visionâ or âmold the publicâs perspectiveâ are asserted rather than probed), so the persuasive depth required for the top band is not consistently reached.

The essay follows a **clear argumentative line of inquiry**: introduction with research question, three themed body sections, and conclusion that synthesises findings. Topic sentences, logical sequencing and cohesive devices (âBuilding onâ¦â) create a mostly fluent structure, although occasional sentence repetition and minor digressions impede full cohesion. Overall organisation is solid and purposeful, aligning with the âwell organised and mostly cohesiveâ descriptor.

Quotations are **frequent, relevant and clearly tied to each paragraphâs claim**; the student draws from all three speeches and occasionally from secondary scholarship. The chosen excerpts directly illustrate the repetitive patterns under discussion, and page locations are supplied. However, some passages are over-long or only partially analysed, and citation style lacks consistency, so the support is effective rather than exemplary.

The student maintains an **academic, formal register** appropriate to an HL essay, using third-person reference and analytical diction. Transitional phrases and disciplinary terminology are employed effectively. Minor slips into conversational tone (âHence,â âas ifâ) and some citation inconsistencies prevent the work from achieving the polish expected in the top band, but style is consistently fitting for the task.

Language is **generally clear and fluent**, with varied sentence structures and accurate vocabulary (e.g. âanaphora,â âhigh modalityâ). Occasional lapsesâmissing commas, redundancy (âIn conclusion, In his speechesâ), or wordinessâdo not seriously hinder comprehension. The overall accuracy and precision align with the âgoodâ rather than âvery highâ level.

The essay moves beyond description by identifying anaphora, parallelism, antithesis and tone, and it **usually links these choices to rhetorical purpose** (unity, urgency, dehumanisation). Yet analysis is uneven: some claims are asserted (âpopulist themeâ) without textual unpacking, and evaluation of effect is often generic (âbrings him closer to the peopleâ). Insightful moments occur but are not sustained, keeping the work in the satisfactory/generally appropriate range rather than the higher âinsightfulâ bands.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and focused, providing a strong foundation for a sustained line of inquiry into Mandelaâs use of repetition.
The heading âHL Essayâ is generic; consider substituting a more descriptive title reflecting your argument to engage the reader immediately.
This introduction situates Mandelaâs three speeches in their historical moment and presents a clear thesis. It demonstrates secure contextual knowledge.
The thesis refers to the âAfrican personâs perspectiveâ but stops short of defining what this entails. Clarify exactly which perspectives you will explore.
Your analysis of collective pronouns is insightful, but the claim about imposing âauthorityâ lacks unpacking. Explain how tone, syntax, or context creates that effect.
Citation style varies between speeches and page references. Adopt a consistent parenthetical format (author, year, page) throughout.
You link âwe pledge ourselvesâ to collective vision effectively; to strengthen this, discuss how this repetition impacted Mandelaâs audience reception.
The sentence here trails off without punctuation or completion. Proofread to ensure all quotations and thoughts are fully presented.
Your identification of parallel structure is sound, but the link to âreflecting the African experienceâ remains abstract. Provide concrete examples of audience impact.
The term âpopulist themeâ is introduced without defining populism or grounding it in Mandelaâs context. Consider elaborating on this concept.
Your analysis of anaphora âAfricans wantâ is accurate; deepen by explaining how this repetition creates solidarity among diverse ethnic groups.
The introductory description of ethnic groups is largely descriptive and interrupts analytical flow. Consider condensing this background or weaving it into your argument more seamlessly.
Your discussion of the antithesis between âwhite supremacyâ and âblack inferiorityâ provides an insightful analysis of moral corruption.
The summary statement that Mandela âencapsulates the African perspectiveâ is generic. Instead, specify which audience response or emotional effect this encapsulation triggers.
The integration of Khumaloâs scholarship enriches context. To strengthen your argument, explicitly connect Khumaloâs critique back to Mandelaâs use of repetition.
Mandelaâs anaphora âthey do notâ is well identified; it convincingly establishes an accusatory tone toward the regime.
The discussion of the Bantu Education Act, while informative, shifts focus away from repetition as a device. Trim or integrate it more directly with your analysis of rhetorical effect.
The conclusion reiterates your main points but would benefit from synthesizing how repetition across speeches advances your research question.
The anaphora of ânoâ in this sentence powerfully conveys desperation. This moment of close textual reading is particularly effective.
The phrase âIn conclusion, In his speechesâ¦â is redundant. Remove one instance of âIn conclusionâ and vary your transition to enhance style.
Your bibliography includes a strong mix of primary and secondary sources, demonstrating effective research.
Several entries lack consistent punctuation and formatting. Apply a uniform citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) throughout your bibliography.
The entry for Mandelaâs 1990 speech omits a publication date and publisher details. Include full bibliographic information for accuracy.
The entry for Meckien repeats institutional names and could be streamlined to avoid redundancy in line with style guidelines.
